response.title: Safer Batteries in 2024: Breakthroughs for Renewables and EVs...
response.snippet: Battery safety improvements are essential for moving the renewable energy transition and electric vehicle adoption.

response.title: Will your electric car burst into flames? A solid‑state battery would...
response.snippet: Solid-state batteries offer a promising new solution. They replace the flammable liquid in existing EV batteries with a solid electrolyte. This reduces the risks of spontaneous combustion when batteries are damaged or when they overheat.

response.title: Introducing Donut Battery: Solid-State EV Battery Revolution | LinkedIn
response.snippet: Today Donut Lab launched the Donut Battery: the world’s first all-solid-state battery in production vehicles. Why is this a big deal? This solves the biggest issues with electric vehicles: range, cost, charging speed, and dependence on rare-earth mat...
response.content: Introducing Donut Battery: Solid-State EV Battery Revolution | Ari Tulla posted on the topic | LinkedIn Skip to main content Introducing Donut Battery: Solid-State EV Battery Revolution This title was summarized by AI from the post below. Ari Tulla 5mo Report this post Today Donut Lab launched the Donut Battery: the world’s first all-solid-state battery in production vehicles. ⚡ Why is this a big deal? This solves the biggest issues with electric vehicles: range, cost, charging speed, and dependence on rare-earth materials. Donut Battery is a better battery than current Lithium-Ion batteries: •⁠ ⁠400 Wh/kg energy density •⁠ ⁠Lower cost than lithium-ion •⁠ ⁠Five-minute full charge and designed for up to 100,000 cycles •⁠ ⁠⁠Made of globally abundant materials The first EV powered by Donut Battery's Verge TS Pro superbike is available to customers, and Donut Lab is working with multiple other OEMs. Donut Battery comes in various sizes and formats. It's suitable for use cases ranging from SUVs to supercars, from drones to heavy machinery, and even for stationary energy storage. Check the launch video below to learn more. https://lnkd.in/dvfCVg_D Kudos Marko Lehtimaki and Tuomo Lehtimäki ! 269 33 Comments Like Comment Share Copy LinkedIn Facebook X Andrew Warren 5mo Report this comment What kind of charging infrastructure is required to get the 5 minute charge time? Can it charge on existing chargers and will it be slower? Solid-state salt batteries are manufactured by printing from nanofluid, which enables the efficient use of space and the production of batteries in varying shapes."
